description: 12-month cumulative return ending 1 month ago (classic skip-1 momentum). Captures medium-term price trend continuation.
notes: Core momentum factor; skips last month to avoid short-term reversal. Low standalone weight due to sector-neutral composite.

description: Prior 1-month return. Often negative predictor (reversal) in cross-section.
notes: XGBoost only. Can be a reversal signal at 1M horizon.

description: 3-month cumulative return. Intermediate-term momentum.
notes: XGBoost only.

description: 6-month cumulative return. Intermediate-term momentum.
notes: XGBoost only.

description: Same as momentum_12m — 12-month return with 1-month skip. XGBoost labels it differently.
notes: Identical to momentum_12m in construction. XGBoost feature name.

description: 12-month rolling standard deviation of monthly returns. Low vol = lower risk-adjusted cost of holding.
notes: XGBoost only. Low-volatility anomaly factor. High vol stocks tend to underperform risk-adjusted.

description: 12-month rolling OLS beta to SPY. Stocks with high beta underperform on risk-adjusted basis (Frazzini & Pedersen 2014).
notes: XGBoost only. Betting-against-beta factor.

description: Equal-weight composite of ROE TTM, neg-accruals, gross profitability, and FCF yield — all sector-neutral z-scored. Captures earnings quality and profitability.
notes: Highest weight in 5-factor model. Sub-components: roe_ttm + neg_accruals + gross_prof + fcf_yield. Sector-neutral.

description: Return on equity — trailing 12-month net income / book equity. Measures management efficiency and capital allocation quality.
notes: Best standalone fundamental signal (Sharpe 0.539). Strong t-stat of 3.1. Core quality signal.

description: Free cash flow / market cap. Measures cash generation efficiency. Combines value and quality — high FCF yield stocks tend to be underpriced relative to cash flow.
notes: FCF signals are noisy but combine value + quality. Large spread between top/bottom quintiles.

description: Negative of (net income - operating cash flow) / total assets. Low accruals = earnings backed by cash flow = higher quality (Sloan 1996). High neg_accruals = good.
notes: Standalone sharpe is low due to high vol (109%) — used as composite sub-component only. Very strong theoretical backing.

description: Revenue / total assets. Novy-Marx (2013) gross profitability measure. Predicts returns even after controlling for value.
notes: Standalone gross_margin sharpe is weak but improves when combined with quality composite.

description: Earnings / price (inverse P/E). Classic value factor. High earnings yield = cheap stock.
notes: Used in engine.py fundamental composite and XGBoost. High vol (53%) reduces standalone sharpe.

description: Revenue / total assets. Measures how efficiently management uses assets to generate sales. High turnover = better operational quality.
notes: Strongest standalone non-ROE fundamental signal. Not in 5-factor model but tested in run_all.py. Sharpe 0.499.

description: Gross profit / revenue. Operating leverage and pricing power indicator. Different from gross_prof (which is vs assets).
notes: Weak standalone. High vol environments dominate. Low t-stat.

description: Negative of debt/equity or debt/assets. Low leverage = financial stability, less bankruptcy risk. Sorted descending so high score = low leverage.
notes: Weak standalone signal — leverage is mostly priced in by market. High vol reduces sharpe.

description: Negative of price-to-book ratio = book-to-market. Classic Fama-French HML factor. Cheap stocks have high book-to-market.
notes: Weakest signal in tests — P/B value has underperformed since 2010 growth dominance. Very high vol (74%). Avoid standalone.

description: Each stock inherits the 3-month relative return (vs SPY) of its GICS sector ETF. Stocks in outperforming sectors score higher. Uses XLC/XLY/XLP/XLE/XLF/XLV/XLI/XLB/XLRE/XLK/XLU.
notes: Equal weight to fund_quality and analyst_rev_3m. Sector momentum is persistent over 1-3 month horizons. Key differentiator.

description: 3-month change in IBES consensus analyst recommendation (1=Strong Buy to 5=Strong Sell, so positive rev_3m = analysts getting more bullish). Leading indicator of earnings surprises.
notes: Equal weight to fund_quality and sector_rs. Analyst upgrades precede price moves. Requires IBES data access.

description: Rolling 36-month OLS beta of sector ETF to WTI crude × rolling z-score of 3-month WTI cumulative return. Positive when oil is rising and the stock's sector has positive oil beta (e.g. XLE). Negative for oil consumers (e.g. airlines) when oil rises.
notes: F5 — newest factor. Lowest weight. Cross-sectionally z-scored so only changes relative rankings. Energy (XLE beta +0.4), Airlines (beta -0.2), Tech (~0.0).

description: Short-period MA / long-period MA - 1. Positive = short MA above long MA = uptrend. Configurable windows (e.g. 3m/12m). Combined with momentum in engine.py technical composite.
notes: Engine.py only. Part of technical composite. Not tested standalone. Parameter-swept in run_all.py technical suite.

description: Rolling 36-month z-score of sector median P/B ratio — negative so cheap sectors score high. Stocks inherit their sector's relative cheapness.
notes: Engine.py only. Requires pb_ratio column in Compustat. Sector-level value contrarian signal.

description: Rolling z-score of high-yield credit spread changes. Negative = spreads narrowing = risk-on (score is negated so high = good). Strong regime overlay signal (t=-2.36 on monthly IC).
notes: Best macro timing signal (Sharpe 0.357, Calmar 0.452 as regime overlay). Used as regime overlay in 5-factor model for invested fraction, not in composite score.

description: Rolling z-score of CPI level. High inflation = headwind for equities (negated in composite). Used in macro regime score.
notes: Second-best macro signal. Negated in macro_regime composite (high inflation = bad for stocks).

description: Rolling z-score of nonfarm payrolls. Hot labor market = Fed tightening risk = headwind (negated). Used in macro regime score.
notes: Weaker macro signal than HY spread or CPI. Can be coincident rather than leading.

description: Rolling z-score of 10Y minus 2Y treasury spread. Inversion (negative) signals recession risk. Used in macro regime.
notes: Weakest standalone macro signal — inversion leads recession by 12-24 months, too early for monthly timing.

description: Rolling z-score of 10Y minus 3M treasury spread. Better near-term recession signal than 10Y-2Y.
notes: Slightly better than 10Y-2Y for timing but still weak standalone. Better as composite with HY spread.

description: Composite of CPIAUCSL_zscore (-1) + PAYEMS_zscore (-1) + hy_spread_widening (-1) = risk-on when all negative (deflating, loose, tightening spreads). Used to scale invested fraction: score<-1→70% invested, score<-0.5→85%, else→100%.
notes: Not a stock-selection factor — regime overlay only. Reduces equity exposure in macro stress. Implemented in five_factor_model.py and engine.py.
